commands.review_summary:
The PR moves Telegram update offsets, sticker cache, sent-message cache, thread bindings, and dispatch replay dedupe from JSON sidecars to plugin-state SQLite stores with legacy import/fallback paths.

PR surface: Source +1146, Tests +955. Total +2101 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a refactor/migration PR rather than a bug report with a failing current-main reproduction path. The relevant proof question is upgrade/runtime behavior after the patch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer compatibility review and contributor-provided real upgrade proof, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: refactor(telegram): persist plugin state in sqlite
target.body:
## Summary

- Move Telegram update offsets, sticker cache, sent-message cache, thread bindings, and message-dispatch replay dedupe onto plugin-state SQLite stores.
- Add legacy JSON import/consumption paths for the moved Telegram state, including runtime-safe fallbacks for offsets, thread bindings, and dispatch dedupe.
- Keep high-volume dispatch replay dedupe bounded by bucketed SQLite rows with plugin-state lock rows, preserving the old logical replay capacity without consuming the whole plugin row cap.
- Keep optional caches best-effort on state failures and preserve remaining TTLs for sent-message rows.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/update-offset-store.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-dispatch-dedupe.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sticker-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/state-migrations.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/token.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.ts extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-dispatch-dedupe.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-dispatch-dedupe.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sent-message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/state-migrations.ts extensions/telegram/src/state-migrations.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sticker-cache-store.ts extensions/telegram/src/sticker-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/update-offset-store.ts extensions/telegram/src/update-offset-store.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/token.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-skills/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin-local state moves from JSON sidecars to plugin-state SQLite stores, with legacy migration/runtime fallback coverage.
Real environment tested: Local focused Telegram Vitest coverage plus extension production/test typechecks and targeted oxlint.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Commands listed in Verification.
Evidence after fix: Focused Telegram suite passed with 8 test files and 225 tests; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: Existing Telegram cache/dedupe/thread/offset behaviors remain covered while new SQLite-backed stores persist and import legacy state.
What was not tested: Live Telegram bot traffic; the ingress spool remains file-backed by design as a crash-recovery queue.
target.author: steipete
